dialogue: Excellent defence, {userName}. Research has the floor.
We traced calls placed minutes after the first strike. Someone reported that the missile reached the base, even though it failed.
So someone was watching for success, not just impact.
Correct. We are matching those signals against hundreds of thousands of IP addresses and satellite passes.
For the algorithm lesson: every route has a cost. On our grid, each move adds 1.
That is why a path of 139 steps survives an end distance of 138, while 138 does not.
One extra step can decide the whole level.
*sirens fill the room before Rick can continue*
They are targeting the GPS system. End distance: {endDistance}.
If GPS falls, the search resets. Protect the data stream.
